title: Static site skeleton: installable/offline PWA shell + per-guide printable output
context: oer-science-labs targets educators in low-resource and low-connectivity settings. The delivery layer must work offline after first load and produce clean printed output per guide, so a teacher with no internet connection can still access and print guides. This task depends on arch-001 (content-format and site-tooling ADRs must be decided before building the site).
objective: Build the static site skeleton using the tooling decided in arch-001: a TypeScript/ESM static site with a service-worker PWA manifest enabling installability and offline use, precache budget and eviction policy, and a client-side PDF/print output path for per-guide printable output (no server round-trip). The skeleton does not need real content; it demonstrates the offline shell and print path working correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Static site builds successfully with the generator and tooling chosen in arch-001
Service worker is registered; site is installable as a PWA; after first visit the shell and precached content load offline (verified by offline E2E)
Precache budget and eviction policy are implemented; the service worker does not cache unbounded content
Per-guide printable/PDF output is generated client-side (no server round-trip required); print CSS produces a readable, standalone printed guide
No backend or app server required; site deploys as static files to the hosting target chosen in arch-001
Accessibility baseline: HTML is semantically valid, skip-navigation and page landmarks are in place, site is keyboard-navigable (full a11y hardening is in a11y-010)
CSP connect-src 'none' header is set; no third-party scripts or trackers are present in the skeleton
output: A pull request adding the static site skeleton (PWA shell, service worker with offline support, per-guide print path) to the oer-science-labs repository.
